import re
import unicodedata


def article_slug(title):
    """Return a URL-safe slug for an article title.

    Converts to lowercase, folds accented characters to ASCII equivalents,
    and joins words with single hyphens.
    """
    decomposed = unicodedata.normalize("NFKD", title)
    ascii_text = decomposed.encode("ascii", "ignore").decode("ascii")
    ascii_text = ascii_text.lower()
    slug = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", ascii_text)
    return slug.strip("-")

from newsroom.text import slugify


def article_slug(title):
    """Return a URL-safe slug for an article title.

    Delegates to the canonical newsroom slugify: lowercases, strips accents,
    drops punctuation, and joins words with single hyphens.
    """
    return slugify(title)

import re
import unicodedata

_PUNCT = re.compile(r"[^\w\s-]", re.UNICODE)
_SPACES = re.compile(r"[\s_-]+")


def article_slug(title):
    """Return a URL-safe slug for an article title: lowercase, words joined by single hyphens."""
    norm = unicodedata.normalize("NFKD", title)
    ascii_only = norm.encode("ascii", "ignore").decode("ascii")
    cleaned = _PUNCT.sub("", ascii_only).strip().lower()
    return _SPACES.sub("-", cleaned).strip("-")

import re


def article_slug(title):
    """Return a URL-safe slug for an article title.

    Converts to lowercase and joins words with single hyphens.
    Non-alphanumeric characters (except hyphens used as separators) are removed.
    """
    # Lowercase the title
    slug = title.lower()
    # Replace any non-alphanumeric characters (except spaces/hyphens) with a space
    slug = re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9\s-]', '', slug)
    # Replace whitespace and hyphens with a single hyphen
    slug = re.sub(r'[\s-]+', '-', slug)
    # Strip leading/trailing hyphens
    slug = slug.strip('-')
    return slug

import re
import unicodedata


def article_slug(title):
    """Return a URL-safe slug for an article title.

    Lowercase, with words joined by single hyphens.
    Accented characters are folded to their ASCII equivalents via NFKD
    decomposition; any non-alphanumeric character is treated as a word
    separator.
    """
    decomposed = unicodedata.normalize("NFKD", title)
    ascii_text = decomposed.encode("ascii", "ignore").decode("ascii")
    ascii_text = ascii_text.lower()
    slug = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", ascii_text)
    return slug.strip("-")

import re


def article_slug(title):
    """Return a URL-safe slug for an article title.

    Converts to lowercase, strips non-alphanumeric characters,
    and joins words with single hyphens.
    """
    # Lowercase the title
    slug = title.lower()
    # Replace non-alphanumeric characters with spaces
    slug = re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]+', ' ', slug)
    # Strip leading/trailing whitespace and replace spaces with hyphens
    slug = slug.strip().replace(' ', '-')
    return slug

import re


def article_slug(title):
    """Return a URL-safe slug for an article title.

    Converts the title to lowercase and joins words with single hyphens,
    stripping any non-alphanumeric characters.
    """
    title = title.lower()
    title = re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9\s-]', '', title)
    title = re.sub(r'[\s-]+', '-', title)
    title = title.strip('-')
    return title

import re
import unicodedata

_PUNCT = re.compile(r"[^\w\s-]", re.UNICODE)
_SPACES = re.compile(r"[\s_-]+")


def article_slug(title):
    """Return a URL-safe slug for an article title: lowercase, words joined by hyphens.

    Folds accented Latin letters to ASCII via NFKD decomposition, drops
    punctuation and emoji, collapses whitespace/underscores/hyphens to a
    single hyphen, and strips leading/trailing hyphens.
    """
    norm = unicodedata.normalize("NFKD", title)
    ascii_only = norm.encode("ascii", "ignore").decode("ascii")
    cleaned = _PUNCT.sub("", ascii_only).strip().lower()
    return _SPACES.sub("-", cleaned).strip("-")

import re
import unicodedata


def article_slug(title):
    """Return a URL-safe slug for an article title.

    Lowercase, with words joined by single hyphens. Accented characters are
    folded to ASCII via NFKD decomposition; any non-alphanumeric character is
    treated as a word separator.
    """
    norm = unicodedata.normalize("NFKD", title)
    ascii_only = norm.encode("ascii", "ignore").decode("ascii")
    lowered = ascii_only.lower()
    slug = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", lowered)
    return slug.strip("-")

from newsroom.text import slugify


def article_slug(title):
    """Return a URL-safe slug for an article title.

    Delegates to newsroom.text.slugify which handles accent folding to ASCII,
    emoji/punctuation stripping, whitespace and hyphen collapsing, and lowercasing.
    Words are joined by single hyphens with no leading, trailing, or repeated hyphens.
    """
    return slugify(title)

import re
import unicodedata

_PUNCT = re.compile(r"[^\w\s-]", re.UNICODE)
_SPACES = re.compile(r"[\s_-]+")


def article_slug(title):
    """Return a URL-safe slug for an article title.

    Lowercases the title, folds accented characters to ASCII via NFKD
    decomposition, strips punctuation, and joins words with single hyphens.
    Leading and trailing hyphens are removed.
    """
    norm = unicodedata.normalize("NFKD", title)
    ascii_only = norm.encode("ascii", "ignore").decode("ascii")
    cleaned = _PUNCT.sub("", ascii_only).strip().lower()
    return _SPACES.sub("-", cleaned).strip("-")

import re
import unicodedata

_PUNCT = re.compile(r"[^\w\s-]", re.UNICODE)
_SPACES = re.compile(r"[\s_-]+")


def article_slug(title):
    """Return a URL-safe slug for an article title.

    Folds accented Latin characters to plain ASCII via NFKD decomposition,
    drops non-ASCII characters (including emoji), collapses whitespace,
    underscores, and hyphens to a single hyphen, then lowercases the result.
    """
    norm = unicodedata.normalize("NFKD", title)
    ascii_only = norm.encode("ascii", "ignore").decode("ascii")
    cleaned = _PUNCT.sub("", ascii_only).strip().lower()
    return _SPACES.sub("-", cleaned).strip("-")

from newsroom.dates import to_iso_z


def render_published_at(dt):
    """Return a display string for an article's published timestamp.

    Follows the newsroom convention: UTC ISO-8601 with a 'Z' suffix,
    second precision, e.g. '2026-06-06T14:30:00Z'.
    """
    return to_iso_z(dt)

from datetime import datetime, timezone


def render_published_at(dt):
    """Return a UTC ISO-8601 string with 'Z' suffix and second precision.

    Follows the newsroom convention: '2026-06-06T14:30:00Z'.
    Never '+00:00', never microseconds.
    Naive datetimes are treated as UTC.
    """
    if dt.tzinfo is None:
        dt = dt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    dt = dt.astimezone(timezone.utc).replace(microsecond=0)
    return dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")

def count_valid(items):
    """Return the number of valid feed items.

    A feed item is valid when it has a non-empty title and url.
    """
    count = 0
    for item in items:
        title = item.get('title', '')
        url = item.get('url', '')
        if title and url:
            count += 1
    return count

def count_valid(items):
    """Return the number of valid feed items.

    A feed item is valid when it has a non-empty title and url.
    """
    count = 0
    for item in items:
        title = item.get("title") if isinstance(item, dict) else getattr(item, "title", None)
        url = item.get("url") if isinstance(item, dict) else getattr(item, "url", None)
        if title and url:
            count += 1
    return count

from newsroom.feed import validate_item
from newsroom.errors import FeedError


def count_valid(items):
    """Return the number of valid feed items.

    A feed item is valid when it has a non-empty title and url.
    """
    count = 0
    for item in items:
        try:
            validate_item(item)
            count += 1
        except FeedError:
            pass
    return count

from newsroom.feed import dedupe_by_url, validate_item
from newsroom.errors import FeedError


def count_valid(items):
    """Return the number of valid feed items.

    An item is valid when it has a non-empty title and url.
    Dedupes by url before counting, per the feed.py contract.
    """
    deduped = dedupe_by_url(items)
    count = 0
    for item in deduped:
        try:
            validate_item(item)
            count += 1
        except FeedError:
            pass
    return count
